questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

According to a country's census, the population (to the nearest million) was 266 in Year 0 and 306 in Year 10. The projected population for Year 50 is 445. To construct a logistic model, both the growth and carrying capacity must be estimated. 
(a) Estimate r by assuming that t = 0 corresponds to Year 0 and that the population between Year 0 and Year 10 is exponential; that is, the population is given by  Round the value of r to four decimal places, if necessary.
(b) Write the solution to the logistic equation using the estimated value of r and use the projected value P(50) = 445 million to find an estimation for the value of the carrying capacity K. Round to the nearest million.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. According to a country's census, the population (to the nearest million) was 266 in Year 0 and 306 in Year 10. The projected population for Year 50 is 445. To construct a logistic model, both the growth and carrying capacity must be estimated. (a) Estimate r by assuming that t = 0 corresponds to Year 0 and that the population between Year 0 and Year 10 is exponential; that is, the population is given by P(t) = P(0)(e) with superscript (rt). Round the value of r to four decimal places, if necessary. (b) Write the solution to the logistic equation using the estimated value of r and use the projected value P(50) = 445 million to find an estimation for the value of the carrying capacity K. Round to the nearest million.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

At a plant that packages bottled spring water, the water is passed through a sequence of ion-exchange filters to reduce the sodium content prior to bottling. Each filter removes 70% of the sodium present in the water passing through it. Determine the number of filters that must be used to reduce the sodium concentration from 30 parts-per-million to 7.20 parts-per-million.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

A company adopts an advertising campaign to weekly add to its customer base. It assumes that as an average fifty percent of its new customers, those added the previous week, will bring in one friend, but those who have been customers longer will not be very effective as recruiters and can be discounted. A media campaign brings in 10,000 customers initially. What is the expected total number of customers with whom the company can expect to have dealings?

It assumes that as an average fifty percent of its new customers, those added the previous week, will bring in one friend, but those who have been customers longer will not be very effective as recruiters and can be discounted. A media campaign brings in 10,000 customers initially. What is the expected total number of customers with whom the company can expect to have dealings?

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

A company makes a very durable product. It sells 20,000 in the first year, but will have diminishing sales due to the product's durability, so that each year it can expect to sell only seventy-five percent of the quantity it will have sold the year before. How many products can the company expect to eventually sell?

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

An object is rolling with a driving force that suddenly ceases. The object then rolls 10 meters in the first second, and in each subsequent interval of time it rolls 80% of the distance it had rolled the second before. This slowing is due to friction. How far will the object eventually roll?
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. An object is rolling with a driving force that suddenly ceases. The object then rolls 10 meters in the first second, and in each subsequent interval of time it rolls 80% of the distance it had rolled the second before. This slowing is due to friction. How far will the object eventually roll?

choices.label: A
choices.html: It will roll an infinite distance.
choices.text: It will roll an infinite distance.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

A child on a swing initially swings through an arc length of 12 meters. The child stops pushing and sits patiently waiting for the swing to stop moving. If friction slows the swing so the length of each arc is 80% of the length of the previous arc, how far will the child have traveled before the swing stops?
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. A child on a swing initially swings through an arc length of 12 meters. The child stops pushing and sits patiently waiting for the swing to stop moving. If friction slows the swing so the length of each arc is 80% of the length of the previous arc, how far will the child have traveled before the swing stops?

choices.label: C
choices.html: The child will travel an infinite distance.
choices.text: The child will travel an infinite distance.

questions.prompt_text: 2) After being in business for t years, a manufacturer of radios is making 1500 + 400t + 30t2 units per year. questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = - sin(πx). What is the period of this function?
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = - sin(πx). What is the period of this function?
questions.solution_html: The period of f is 2.
questions.solution_text: The period of f is 2. Short Description: A graph shows a sinusoidal periodic function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5 to 5 in increments of 0.5, and the y axis ranges from negative 1 to 1 in increments of 0.5. The curve slopes upward from (negative 5, 0) to (negative 4.5, 1), slopes downward to (negative 4, 0), slopes down further to (negative 3.5, negative 1), and slopes upward to (negative 3, 0). The cycle repeats.

feedback_html.solution: The period of f is 2.

Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5 to 5 in increments of 0.5, and the y axis ranges from negative 1 to 1 in increments of 0.5. The cycle repeats.

questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = tan (x/2). What is the period of this function?
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = tan (x/2). What is the period of this function?
questions.solution_html: period = 2π
questions.solution_text: period = 2π Short Description: A graph of a periodic function shows a tangent function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5.5 pi to 5.5 pi in increments of pi over 2, and the y axis ranges from negative 9 to 9 in increments of 1. The period of the function is 2 pi, so the curve repeats itself after each 2 pi interval on the x axis. The curve starts from the left at negative 5.5 pi and rises through the point (negative 5.5 pi, 1) and (negative 5 pi, infinity), then a new tangent curve again starts from (negative 5 pi, negative infinity), and rises through points (negative 4.5 pi, 1), (negative 3.5 pi, 1), and (negative 3 pi, infinity). The curve repeats itself in the same manner on the entire x axis to the right of (5.5 pi, negative 1). The asymptotes to the curves are parallel to the y axis and they pass through points (negative 3 pi, 0), (negative pi, 0), (pi, 0), and (3 pi, 0).

feedback_html.solution: period = 2π

images.alt: Short Description: A graph of a periodic function shows a tangent function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 5.5 pi to 5.5 pi in increments of pi over 2, and the y axis ranges from negative 9 to 9 in increments of 1. The period of the function is 2 pi, so the curve repeats itself after each 2 pi interval on the x axis. The curve starts from the left at negative 5.5 pi and rises through the point (negative 5.5 pi, 1) and (negative 5 pi, infinity), then a new tangent curve again starts from (negative 5 pi, negative infinity), and rises through points (negative 4.5 pi, 1), (negative 3.5 pi, 1), and (negative 3 pi, infinity). The curve repeats itself in the same manner on the entire x axis to the right of (5.5 pi, negative 1). The asymptotes to the curves are parallel to the y axis and they pass through points (negative 3 pi, 0), (negative pi, 0), (pi, 0), and (3 pi, 0).

questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = -2 cos. What is the period of the function? What is the amplitude?
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = -2 cos (3x + (π/6)) . What is the period of the function? What is the amplitude?
questions.solution_html: period = , amplitude = 2
questions.solution_text: period = (2π/3) , amplitude = 2 Short Description: A graph of a periodic cosine function as a sinusoidal curve. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 2.75 pi to 2.5 pi in increments of pi over 4, and the y axis ranges from negative 3.75 to 3.75 in increments of 0.75. The amplitude of the curve is 2, so the entire curve lies between negative 2 and 2 on the y axis with a period of 2 pi over 3. The curve starts from the left of (negative 2.75 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 2.39 pi, 2) then falls through points (negative 2.22 pi, 0), reaches its minimum at (negative 2.06 pi, 2) and then rises through the point (negative 1.89 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 1.72 pi, 2). The curve repeats itself in the same fashion with a period of 2 pi over 3 and continues to the right of 2 pi on the positive x axis.

feedback_html.solution: period = , amplitude = 2

The amplitude of the curve is 2, so the entire curve lies between negative 2 and 2 on the y axis with a period of 2 pi over 3. questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = |sin x|, - 2π ≤ x ≤ 2π.
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of the function f(x) = |sin x|, - 2π ≤ x ≤ 2π.
questions.solution_text: Short Description: A graph of a periodic function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 2.5 pi to 2.5 pi in increments of pi over 4. The y axis ranges from negative 0.75 to 1.75 in increments of 0.25. The amplitude of the curve is 1 and entire curve lies above the x axis. The curve starts from the point (negative 2 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 1.5 pi, 1) then falls to the point (negative pi, 0), and again reaches its maximum at (negative 0.5 pi, 1). The period of the curve is pi, so the curve repeats itself after each pi interval on the x axis and terminates at point (2 pi, 0).

images.alt: Short Description: A graph of a periodic function. Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 2.5 pi to 2.5 pi in increments of pi over 4. The y axis ranges from negative 0.75 to 1.75 in increments of 0.25. The amplitude of the curve is 1 and entire curve lies above the x axis. The curve starts from the point (negative 2 pi, 0), reaches its maximum at (negative 1.5 pi, 1) then falls to the point (negative pi, 0), and again reaches its maximum at (negative 0.5 pi, 1). The period of the curve is pi, so the curve repeats itself after each pi interval on the x axis and terminates at point (2 pi, 0).

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

A manufacturer uses raw materials to produce p products each day. Suppose that each delivery of a particular material is $d, whereas the storage of that material is x dollars per unit stored per day. (One unit is the amount required to produce one product). How much should be delivered every x days to minimize the average daily cost in the production cycle between deliveries?
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. A manufacturer uses raw materials to produce p products each day. Suppose that each delivery of a particular material is $d, whereas the storage of that material is x dollars per unit stored per day. (One unit is the amount required to produce one product). How much should be delivered every x days to minimize the average daily cost in the production cycle between deliveries?
questions.solution_html:
If he asks for a delivery every x days, then he must order (px) to have enough material for that delivery cycle. The average amount in storage is approximately one-half of the delivery amount, or . Thus, the cost of delivery and storage for each cycle is approximately
 Cost per cycle = delivery costs + storage costs
 Cost per cycle = d +  ∙ x
We compute the average daily cost c(x) by dividing the cost per cycle by the number of days x in the cycle.
 c(x) =  + 
We find the critical points by determining where the derivative is equal to zero.
 c'(x) = -  +  = 0
 x = ± 
Therefore, an absolute minimum occurs at  days.
questions.solution_text: If he asks for a delivery every x days, then he must order (px) to have enough material for that delivery cycle. The average amount in storage is approximately one-half of the delivery amount, or (px/2) . Thus, the cost of delivery and storage for each cycle is approximately Cost per cycle = delivery costs + storage costs Cost per cycle = d + (px/2) ∙ x We compute the average daily cost c(x) by dividing the cost per cycle by the number of days x in the cycle. c(x) = (d/x) + (px/2) We find the critical points by determining where the derivative is equal to zero. c'(x) = - (d/(x) with superscript (2)) + (p/2) = 0 x = ± square root of ((2d/p)) Therefore, an absolute minimum occurs at square root of ((2d/p)) days.
